projectDescription: A truly independent, from-scratch web browser with its own rendering engine, JavaScript engine, and multi-process architecture, targeting Linux and macOS.
keyFeatures: Multi-process architecture: separate UI, WebContent, RequestServer, and ImageDecoder processes
LibWeb: from-scratch HTML/CSS rendering engine implementing WHATWG/W3C standards
LibWasm: WebAssembly implementation written from scratch
Cross-platform: Linux, macOS, Windows (via WSL2), Android
Multiple UI frontends: Qt6, AppKit (macOS native), GTK4, Android
Firefox DevTools integration via remote debugging protocol
AK standard library replacing C++ STL with custom primitives
publicApiSurface: Meta/ladybird.py — primary build and run script
WebView::ViewImplementation — interface for creating new browser UI frontends
WebView::Application — base class for UI-specific command-line flags
LibWeb — web rendering engine (HTML, CSS, DOM, JS bindings)
LibCore::EventLoop — event loop and OS abstraction
LibTLS / LibCrypto — TLS and cryptography primitives
Meta/WPT.sh — Web Platform Test runner script
